headline: Arm CEO Haas in line to lead much of parent SoftBank's international business, FT reports
summary: Rene Haas, the CEO of chip designer Arm, is in line to lead much of parent ‌SoftBank's international business while keeping his current role at Arm, ‌the Financial Times reported on Thursday, citing people familiar with the matter.  SoftBank did not ​immediately respond to a request for comment from Reuters, while Arm, which is majority-owned by the Japanese conglomerate, declined to comment.  Haas's appointment aims to push forward ‌SoftBank's AI chip strategy, ⁠Project Izanagi, launched to compete with rivals such as U.S.-based chipmaker Nvidia, according to the FT report.

content.title: Arm CEO Haas in line to lead much of parent SoftBank's international business, FT reports
content.summary: Rene Haas, the CEO of chip designer Arm, is in line to lead much of parent ‌SoftBank's international business while keeping his current role at Arm, ‌the Financial Times reported on Thursday, citing people familiar with the matter.  SoftBank did not ​immediately respond to a request for comment from Reuters, while Arm, which is majority-owned by the Japanese conglomerate, declined to comment.  Haas's appointment aims to push forward ‌SoftBank's AI chip strategy, ⁠Project Izanagi, launched to compete with rivals such as U.S.-based chipmaker Nvidia, according to the FT report.

headline: EDGX launches first in-orbit demonstration of its AI computing system on SpaceX Transporter-16
summary: Belgian spacetech EDGX has successfully launched its first in-orbit demonstration of STERNA, an AI-powered edge computer for satellite constellations aboard SpaceX's Transporter-16 mission. With two hosted payloads now in orbit, EDGX enables real-time data processing directly in space, a critical capability for next-generation satellite constellations across commercial, governmental, and defence applications.

headline: CoreWeave and Meta Announce $21 Billion Expanded AI Infrastructure Agreement
summary: LIVINGSTON, N.J., April 09, 2026--CoreWeave (Nasdaq: CRWV), the Essential Cloud for AI™, today announced an expanded, long-term agreement with Meta Platforms, Inc. to provide AI cloud capacity through December 2032 for approximately $21 billion. With this deal, the two companies are continuing their existing relationship, increasing support for Meta’s development and deployment of AI.

content.title: CoreWeave and Meta Announce $21 Billion Expanded AI Infrastructure Agreement
content.summary: LIVINGSTON, N.J., April 09, 2026--CoreWeave (Nasdaq: CRWV), the Essential Cloud for AI™, today announced an expanded, long-term agreement with Meta Platforms, Inc. to provide AI cloud capacity through December 2032 for approximately $21 billion. With this deal, the two companies are continuing their existing relationship, increasing support for Meta’s development and deployment of AI.

headline: Nvidia Shares Near Level Where Technical Traders See a Breakout
summary: (Bloomberg) -- After months of sluggish returns, Nvidia Corp.’s stock is rallying again and close to breaking out of its narrow trading range, which market technicians see as a bullish signal. Shares of the chip giant are up more than 10% in the past six sessions, their longest winning streak since October. This comes after substantial stretch of nothing, with the shares essentially flat from September 2025 through the end of last month. They closed trading Wednesday at $182, near the $185 level

content.title: Nvidia Shares Near Level Where Technical Traders See a Breakout
content.summary: (Bloomberg) -- After months of sluggish returns, Nvidia Corp.’s stock is rallying again and close to breaking out of its narrow trading range, which market technicians see as a bullish signal. Shares of the chip giant are up more than 10% in the past six sessions, their longest winning streak since October. This comes after substantial stretch of nothing, with the shares essentially flat from September 2025 through the end of last month. They closed trading Wednesday at $182, near the $185 level
